response.justification: While the candidate brings valuable geospatial domain expertise and exemplary software craftsmanship practices, they lack the critical technical requirement: modern C++ or Rust proficiency. Their C++ experience ended in 2014-2015 (11+ years ago), and their recent decade has focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and web systems. A routing tiling role demands systems-level programming, performance optimization, and high-frequency data processing—capabilities not evident in their current trajectory.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain knowledge from founding GISCollective and Green Map work
Proven ability to rapidly master multiple programming languages and technology stacks
Exceptional testing culture and software craftsmanship practices (TDD, workshops, open-source test frameworks)
concerns: C++ experience is over a decade old; most recent 10+ years concentrated on TypeScript and web development
No demonstrated systems programming, performance optimization, or routing/navigation algorithm experience
Current career trajectory (TypeScript/Node.js/web systems) diverges significantly from required systems-level C++/Rust work
